description: A plain screenshot of a news article headline and byline on a white background, with no added agency graphic design, seal, image, or border. Large bold black sans-serif headline reads: 'Many high school graduates don't finish college. Local unions are investing in paid apprenticeships.' Beneath it is the byline 'By Claire Partain, Staff Writer' (the writer's name in red) and the date 'Sep 3, 2025.' Because it is simply a cropped article screenshot rather than a stylized graphic, it is marked not designed. DOL uses it (per the tweet) as evidence that a grassroots 'Make America Skilled Again' movement to expand apprenticeships is growing. Technique: amplifying favorable press coverage. Target audience: general public, students, and workforce stakeholders.
notable_text: Many high school graduates don't finish college. Local unions are investing in paid apprenticeships. / By Claire Partain, Staff Writer / Sep 3, 2025
